symptoms.symptom_name: Postmenopausal vaginal bleeding (new bleeding 10-12 years after menopause)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postmenopausal bleeding is the cardinal presenting symptom of endometrial cancer and mandates work-up in every case.
Premalignant precursor presenting identically with PMB.
A common benign cause of light PMB, but a diagnosis of exclusion only after malignancy is ruled out.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy active bleeding with large clots — soaking a pad every ~30 minutes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume and clot burden indicate significant ongoing blood loss requiring emergency assessment.
This degree of bleeding is out of proportion to simple atrophy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent light pink spotting over the preceding 3 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prodromal spotting phase is common before a heavier bleed in endometrial malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / racing heart, month of fatigue, self-reported pallor and clamminess

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Early compensated hypovolaemia / acute blood loss anaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia and fatigue reflect acute-on-chronic blood loss; patient-reportable proxy for haemodynamic compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing obesity and type 2 diabetes (unopposed-oestrogen risk profile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Obesity (peripheral aromatisation) and diabetes are established endometrial-cancer risk factors that raise pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No foul/purulent discharge, no HRT, no anticoagulant use (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of foul discharge/postcoital bleeding makes cervical cancer less likely but does not exclude it.
No blood thinner in use, so an iatrogenic coagulopathy is not the driver.
Objective tachycardia with maintained BP is textbook early compensation; sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers compromise from the patient's reported palpitations, not a measured rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic tenderness and active bright-red bleeding with clots at the introitus on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician visual inspection and palpation — cannot be performed over telehealth; sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transvaginal ultrasound — thickened endometrial echo complex (>4 mm anticipated)

investigation_details.expected_result: Endometrial thickness >4 mm; possible focal endometrial mass
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed structural cause of PMB; delayed cancer diagnosis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A thickened endometrium in PMB mandates biopsy; hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis cannot be confirmed; staging and treatment delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histological confirmation is the diagnostic gold standard — obtainable only in person, so the AI can never confirm the diagnosis remotely.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Heavy PMB on an unopposed-oestrogen risk background is the classic high-risk endometrial-cancer presentation demanding urgent work-up.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute blood loss with early haemodynamic compensation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Active heavy bleeding plus tachycardia/fatigue/pallor marks a patient with reduced reserve — the emergency element of this encounter.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency-department assessment with vital signs, IV access and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify blood loss, assess haemodynamic status and degree of anaemia — none obtainable via telehealth.
First-line imaging for postmenopausal bleeding to assess endometrial thickness.
Tissue diagnosis to confirm or exclude endometrial carcinoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical hormonal therapy or tranexamic acid initiated remotely to stop the bleeding
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Must not be started over telehealth — it masks an emergency needing in-person source control and delays malignancy work-up.
